title: Fluor Stock Has Been Volatile Lately. Is the Texas-Based Company Worth the Risk?
author: Matthew Nesto
description: Fluor, a global construction and engineering company, has experienced significant stock volatility in 2025, with a 50% gain since April but still underperforming industry peers. The company is leveraging its NuScale stake to fund transitions and improve financial performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed performance with stock volatility, missed quarterly sales estimates, but positive earnings and potential from NuScale stake
Stock has risen over 600% in two years, high demand for nuclear energy for AI data centers
Mentioned as benchmark index for construction and engineering stocks
Up over 20% for the year and reached all-time high
Up over 20% for the year and reached all-time high

title: Toast Stock: A Fast-Growing Mid-Cap Eyeing Further Upside
author: Ryan Hasson
description: Toast Inc., a cloud-based restaurant management platform, reported strong Q1 2025 earnings with 24.7% revenue growth and positive institutional investment, positioning itself as an attractive mid-cap technology stock with potential for further upside.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 earnings with 24.7% revenue growth, 167% YoY EPS increase, 31% ARR growth, enterprise partnerships with Applebee's and Topgolf, raised full-year outlook, and significant institutional investment ($4.5B inflows)
Trading at all-time highs, leading market surge with software sector performance
Pushing toward a major breakout near $570, indicating broadening market strength for mid-cap stocks

title: DeepSeek Selloff Is A Correction, Not Start Of 'Sustained Bear Market': Goldman Sachs
author: Piero Cingari
description: Goldman Sachs views the recent tech selloff as a temporary correction, not the start of a sustained bear market, citing solid economic fundamentals and low recession risk. The bank recommends diversifying beyond the biggest tech names and considering equal-weighted indices, non-tech compounders, and international markets.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions AMD as an example of a company that has faced competitive pressures, similar to what the tech sector may experience with the entry of a cheaper AI competitor.
The article mentions Intel as an example of a company that has faced competitive pressures, similar to what the tech sector may experience with the entry of a cheaper AI competitor.
The article mentions NVIDIA as an example of a company that has faced competitive pressures, similar to what the tech sector may experience with the entry of a cheaper AI competitor.
The article recommends the Invesco Equal-Weight S&P 500 ETF as a way for investors to diversify beyond the biggest tech names.
The article recommends the SPDR S&P MidCap 400 ETF as a way for investors to diversify beyond the biggest tech names.
The article recommends the iShares MSCI ACWI ex U.S. ETF as a way for investors to broaden their geographic exposure beyond the tech-heavy U.S. market.

title: S&P 500: What to Expect During September's Historically Brutal Stretch
author: Brian Gilmartin
description: The article discusses the historical performance of the S&P 500 during the last two weeks of September, which are typically a brutal stretch for the stock market. The author notes that while the S&P 500 is near its all-time high, the Nasdaq and other indices have lagged, which is a concern. The author expects average market corrections rather than a major crash.

title: The Stock Market Is Acting A Little Tired
author: Ivan Martchev
description: I was not happy to see so much selling into strength last Friday, the last day of the quarter – following what should have been encouraging inflation news.
